topic: Someone finds an unsigned birthday card in their mailbox
raw_output:
[story 1]

The mail slot rattled with a dull thud, a sound that always preceded another unwelcome intrusion. I dragged myself to the door, the weight of the day pressing down on my shoulders like a wet blanket. There, tucked beneath a stack of junk flyers, was a card. Unsigned. My gaze snagged on the crisp white paper, and a familiar tightening began in my chest, a dull, heavy ache. It was a birthday card, clearly meant for someone, but the recipient remained a blank space. I picked it up, the edges slightly rough against my fingertips. Why was there always something left unsaid? A flicker of something sharp crossed my face before I quickly shoved it back into the box, the paper crinkling loudly in the silence of my cramped apartment.

[story 2]

I hated the way the afternoon light slanted through the window, illuminating the dust motes dancing in the air—a perfect metaphor for my current mood. Another day, another reminder of obligations I didn't want to fulfill. When I finally retrieved the mail, my movements were sluggish, heavy. The card was sitting there, stark white against the dull cardboard. It was addressed simply to 'Birthday Person.' A strange, prickly sensation crawled up my neck. I stared at the lack of a name, a void where connection should have been. My jaw clenched so hard I could feel the muscle twitching beneath my skin. I didn't want to open it; I just wanted the whole thing to disappear.

[story 3]

The sheer volume of correspondence felt like an assault. Every piece of paper seemed to carry an unspoken expectation, a demand for warmth I simply wasn't equipped to give. I shuffled through the pile with practiced indifference, my eyes scanning for anything remotely interesting. Then I saw it—a card, pristine and utterly devoid of personalization. It sat there, an anomaly in the sea of expected greetings. A slow, bitter heat rose in my throat. Who was this person, and why were they sending something so impersonal? I felt a sudden, intense urge to tear it up, to destroy the evidence of this careless gesture, but my hand hesitated mid-air, trembling slightly before I let it drop.
